alpha.definition: cov/var (ddof=1); alpha = (mean r - beta mean b) x P, rf=0

anova.definition: one-way F from raw rows; p via deterministic incomplete beta (scipy f_oneway)

beta.definition: cov/var (ddof=1); alpha = (mean r - beta mean b) x P, rf=0

cdar.definition: mean of drawdowns at/beyond the (1−level) drawdown quantile (Chekhlov-Uryasev)

chi_square.definition: statistic \| p-no-yates \| statistic-no-yates | independence test from RAW observation pairs; Yates only when df==1 (scipy)

churn_rate.definition: retention | churned/total from raw flags; retention = 1 - churn

confidence_interval.definition: CI half-width of the mean: crit * s/sqrt(n)

cornish_fisher_var.definition: Gaussian VaR with the Cornish-Fisher skew/kurtosis z-expansion

correlation.definition: spearman | fsum-centered Pearson; Spearman = Pearson on midranks (ties averaged)

downside_deviation.definition: std (ddof=1) x sqrt(P); downside uses sqrt(mean(min(r,0)^2))

duplicate_count.definition: rows duplicating an earlier row (pandas duplicated keep='first')

ece.definition: Guo et al. equal-width (lo,hi] bins, sum (n_b/n)\|acc_b - conf_b\|

effect_size.definition: hedges_g \| glass_delta | pooled-SD d; Hedges' exact-gamma J; Glass = control SD

fisher_exact.definition: exact two-sided hypergeometric via integer combinatorics (scipy semantics)

gini_norm.definition: max ECDF gap between class score distributions; 2*AUC-1

groupby_aggregate.definition: mean \| sum:`<group>` \| mean:`<group>` | per-group fsum/mean; no group label -> degenerate (groups kept in terms)

information_ratio.definition: mean(r-b)/std(r-b) x sqrt(P); std(r-b) x sqrt(P)

irr.definition: rate where npv=0, deterministic bisection; no sign change -> degenerate

join_row_loss.definition: len(left) - len(joined); 0 = lossless, negative = fan-out; use `left.csv::id` cross-artifact binding

ks_statistic.definition: max ECDF gap between class score distributions; 2*AUC-1

ks_test.definition: classical Kolmogorov asymptotic p (kstwobign; documented vs scipy's finite-n refinement)

kurtosis.definition: biased g1; biased excess g2 (scipy defaults)

log_loss.definition: clip | -mean(y ln p + (1-y) ln(1-p)); exact mode -> inf is degenerate, clip = legacy 1e-15

map_at_k.definition: AP@k with min(R,k) denominator (documented), zero-relevant skipped

mape.definition: smape | mean(\|p-a\|/\|a\|); zero actual -> degenerate (no epsilon fudge); smape = mean(2\|p-a\|/(\|p\|+\|a\|))

martin_ratio.definition: annualized return / ulcer index (Ulcer Performance Index)

mase.definition: MAE scaled by in-sample naive-m MAE (Hyndman & Koehler)

max_drawdown.definition: worst peak-to-trough of the equity curve (path-dependent)

max_drawdown_duration.definition: longest consecutive run below the running peak (periods)

mcc.definition: Gorodkin multiclass MCC, exact integer sums; 0.0 on zero denominator (sklearn)

npv.definition: sum cf_t/(1+r)^t, cf[0] at t=0 (numpy-financial); no rate -> degenerate

omega_ratio.definition: count(r>0)/n; sum gains/\|sum losses\|; sum max(r-t,0)/sum max(t-r,0)

p_value.definition: pooled \| z | two-sided two-sample p via deterministic incomplete beta / erfc

perplexity.definition: exp(-mean(logprob)), natural log; positive logprobs degrade

point_biserial.definition: Pearson r between a 0/1 and a continuous column (SciPy pointbiserialr)

pr_auc.definition: trapezoid | sklearn AP step integral (ties grouped)

profit_factor.definition: count(r>0)/n; sum gains/\|sum losses\|; sum max(r-t,0)/sum max(t-r,0)

reconciliation_total.definition: sum(a) - sum(b); 0 = reconciled; use `ledger.csv::col` cross-artifact binding

skewness.definition: biased g1; biased excess g2 (scipy defaults)

sortino.definition: mean / downside-dev x sqrt(P), target 0, full-sample denominator

test_coverage.definition: lines with hits>0 / lines, from raw per-line hit counts

theil_sen_slope.definition: median of all pairwise slopes (SciPy theilslopes)

tracking_error.definition: mean(r-b)/std(r-b) x sqrt(P); std(r-b) x sqrt(P)

volatility.definition: std (ddof=1) x sqrt(P); downside uses sqrt(mean(min(r,0)^2))

win_rate.definition: count(r>0)/n; sum gains/\|sum losses\|; sum max(r-t,0)/sum max(t-r,0)
